title: 10 best herbal creams for congestive heart failure

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, thereby alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Cardiac glycosides: Some herbal turmeric creams contain cardiac glycosides, such as digoxin, which have been shown to improve heart function by increasing the force of contractions and reducing congestion in the lungs, thereby helping to manage congestive heart failure symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory lignans: Turmeric's lignan content has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to congestive heart failure. By reducing these factors, turmeric creams may help alleviate symptoms such as swelling, shortness of breath, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are major contributors to heart failure. By reducing inflammation and promoting cellular repair, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure such as swelling, fatigue, and shortness of breath. This natural remedy may also help improve heart function and overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has shown to have cardiovascular protective effects by reducing inflammation, improving nitric oxide production, and preventing endothelial dysfunction, all of which can help manage congestive heart failure.

Beta-Caryophyllene: Beta-Caryophyllene has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, as well as improve cardiac function and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes contain curcumin, a polyphenol with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce heart failure symptoms.
Leaves: Curcuma longa leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their potential cardiovascular benefits and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots contain turmeric's active compound curcumin, which has been studied for its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2-3 inches of fresh rhizome into a bowl for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of grated rhizome with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until oil turns golden brown.
Strain the mixture into a bowl lined with cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Whip the mixture with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Cardioprotective effects: Ginseng has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease and improving cardiovascular function.

Blood flow enhancement: Ginseng is known to dilate blood vessels, improve blood circulation, and reduce blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, shortness of breath, and swelling associated with congestive heart failure.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties may help neutralize oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of cardiovascular disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its potential to improve cardiovascular function and reduce symptoms associated with the condition. The cream may help to increase blood flow to the heart and enhance its pumping efficiency, thereby alleviating fatigue and shortness of breath. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Panax ginseng may help to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall cardiovascular health, contributing to improved heart function and quality of life for individuals with congestive heart failure.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides, particularly Rb1 and Rg1, have been shown to have a cardioprotective effect by improving heart function and reducing inflammation, which helps alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid saponin, are known for their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. They help to improve blood flow to the heart, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Glycosides: Glycosides, specifically the ginsenosides, have been shown to have a positive inotropic effect, meaning they increase the heart's contractility and pump blood more efficiently, which helps to alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have cardiovascular benefits.
Leaves: They contain ginsenosides and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular function.
Stems: They also contain ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried panax ginseng roots and soak them in 500ml of water for 2 hours.
Filter the liquid using a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a ginseng extract.
Combine 300g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt at 180-190°f.
Add 100g of ginseng extract and 50g of shea butter to the melted wax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds help to improve cardiac function by increasing the force of contractions, reducing blood pressure, and preventing arrhythmias (irregular heartbeats), all of which can alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, have been shown to improve blood vessel function, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of heart failure and its complications.

Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to protect against cellular damage caused by free radicals, which can accumulate in the body and exacerbate congestive heart failure; anthocyanins may also help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its unique ability to promote cardiovascular health. The bioactive compounds in hawthorn have been shown to improve blood flow, lower blood pressure, and strengthen the heart's pumping ability. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system. By addressing these underlying factors, hawthorn creams may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, such as shortness of breath and fatigue, and support overall heart health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Crataegus monogyna creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the heart, thereby alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids like oleanolic acid and ursolic acid in Crataegus monogyna have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the heart from damage, improve cardiac function, and reduce the risk of complications associated with congestive heart failure.

Saponins: Saponins present in Crataegus monogyna creams, such as crataegus saponins, have been found to have cardiotonic properties, which may help improve cardiac function, increase the strength of heart contractions, and enhance the heart's ability to pump blood, ultimately alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their flavonoid and antioxidant content which can help protect the heart.
Fruits: Used for their flavonoid and antioxidant content which can help improve cardiovascular health.
Barks: Used due to their flavonoid and antioxidant content which can help protect the heart.
remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna berries in late summer or early fall when they are ripe and fully colored.
Dry the berries in a single layer on paper towels for 7 to 10 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of the berry powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container and let cool completely.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal garlic creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of congestive heart failure.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in herbal garlic creams, such as allicin, can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which is beneficial for individuals with congestive heart failure who often experience decreased cardiac output and increased peripheral resistance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal garlic creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the progression of congestive heart failure by promoting fibrosis (scarring) in the heart muscle and reducing contractility. By reducing inflammation, herbal garlic creams may help slow or stop the progression of disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin and diallyl disulfide, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure, preventing platelet aggregation, and improving blood vessel function. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may contribute to heart failure, while its anticoagulant properties can help prevent blood clots. These benefits can aid in reducing the severity of congestive heart failure symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin has been shown to help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health by inhibiting platelet aggregation and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to congestive heart failure.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in garlic, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of terpene in garlic, have been found to have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, potentially alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like allicin, which may help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Contain saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may benefit heart health.
Buds: Contain compounds that have been found to have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 allium sativum bulbs in the early morning when the soil is still moist.
Peel the bulbs and extract the juice using a hydraulic press at 500 pounds of pressure.
Combine the extracted juice with 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh filter and store the resulting cream in an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to congestive heart failure by reducing cardiac tissue damage and improving blood flow.

Vasodilation and improved circulation: Ginger's bioactive compounds, including gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure such as fatigue, swelling, and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's high antioxidant capacity helps protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular function. By reducing inflammation in the blood vessels and improving blood flow, ginger creams may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, such as shortness of breath and fatigue. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties may help protect against cell damage and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, contributing to the management of congestive heart failure.

Gingerene: Gingerene has antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cellular damage and improve cardiovascular function, ultimately supporting heart health and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Volatile oil (Zingiberene): Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene found in ginger, has vasodilatory effects, which may help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which may help mitigate some of the cardiovascular issues associated with congestive heart failure.

Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale are used for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to heart health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes 3-4 inches long and 1 inch wide.
Clean and peel the rhizomes then slice them into thin pieces 1/8 inch thick.
Combine the sliced rhizomes with 1 quart of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes or until the liquid reduces to 1/2 cup.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a mixing bowl then whip in 1/2 cup of shea butter.

remedy_properties: Cardiotonic properties: Herbal olive creams containing cardiotonic herbs like Digitalis purpurea or Strophanthus gratus help to strengthen the heart's contractions and improve its efficiency in pumping blood, which is essential for patients with congestive heart failure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Olive cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as oleocanthal and squalene, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying factor in congestive heart failure. By reducing inflammation, these creams can alleviate symptoms like swelling and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich components of olive cream, including vitamin E, polyphenols, and flavonoids, help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which are thought to contribute to the progression of congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Olea europaea, also known as olive, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's high levels of oleuropein and other polyphenols have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart. This can help to improve heart function, decrease fluid buildup, and enhance overall cardiovascular well-being. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: This polyphenolic compound has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the heart, thereby alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Hydroxytyrosol: This phenolic compound has been found to have antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties, which can help protect the heart from oxidative stress and cell death, thereby improving cardiac function and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, thereby improving cardiac function and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and polyphenols, which help to protect the heart and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Contain oleuropein, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of heart disease.
Fruits: Rich in oleic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and may help to improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh olea europaea leaves at dawn when they are still covered in dew.
Steep the harvested leaves in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil with the infused oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a double boiler and heat it to 40 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of bioactive compound found in maidenhair tree (Ginkgo biloba), have been shown to improve cardiovascular function and reduce cardiac hypertrophy by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in cardiac fibrosis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in maidenhair tree creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with congestive heart failure, thereby reducing cardiac damage and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, another type of bioactive compound found in maidenhair tree extracts, have been demonstrated to exhibit antiplatelet and anticoagulant activities, which can help reduce the risk of blood clots and improve blood flow, ultimately alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress. This leads to enhanced oxygen delivery to the heart and surrounding tissues. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, such as shortness of breath and fatigue, and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to improved heart function in congestive heart failure.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, are phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce blood pressure, improve blood flow, and decrease the risk of cardiac arrhythmias, all of which are beneficial for congestive heart failure patients.

Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, a class of diterpene trilactones, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, contributing to improved heart function and reduced risk of cardiac complications in congestive heart failure patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have beneficial effects on cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Ginkgo seed extract is sometimes used in combination with leaf extract, as it is thought to enhance the overall efficacy of the treatment.
Barks: Some traditional remedies use Ginkgo bark, which is believed to contain compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Slowly add the infused oil to the beeswax mixture and stir until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Ashwagandha cream helps reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to CHF symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and palpitations, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Antioxidant and Inflammation-Reducing Properties: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that damage the heart and surrounding tissues, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can exacerbate CHF symptoms.

Cardioprotective and Vasodilatory Properties: Ashwagandha cream has been shown to improve cardiac function by increasing blood flow and reducing peripheral resistance, which can help alleviate CHF symptoms such as shortness of breath and swelling in the legs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It helps to improve cardiovascular function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are major contributors to heart failure. Ashwagandha cream also enhances nitric oxide production, which improves blood flow and reduces blood pressure. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, reducing the strain on the heart and promoting overall cardiovascular health. This makes it a potential natural remedy for congestive heart failure.
remedy_constituents: Withaferin A: This alkaloid helps with congestive heart failure by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart failure progression.

Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help alleviate congestive heart failure symptoms by reducing cardiac fibrosis (scarring) and improving cardiac function, likely due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Withanoside IV: This saponin helps manage congestive heart failure by exerting vasodilatory effects, which can improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, thereby alleviating cardiac workload and improving heart function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams due to their high content of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate heart failure symptoms.
Leaves: They are used to make creams because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
Fruits: They are used to make creams due to their rich content of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that can help protect the heart and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of withania somnifera roots from a trusted supplier and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to ensure proper preservation.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered withania somnifera with 100 grams of a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a 2:1 ratio.
Combine the herbal mixture with 100 grams of a base cream such as shea butter or beeswax and stir until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure such as swelling and fluid buildup.
Antioxidant properties: Frankincense has antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to heart disease and congestive heart failure.

Cardiac glycoside-like activity: Some studies have shown that frankincense extracts have a similar effect on the heart as cardiac glycosides, such as digitalis, by increasing the force of contraction and reducing heart rate, which can help improve cardiac function in individuals with congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's boswellic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to congestive heart failure. By reducing inflammation, boswellia serrata cream may help improve blood flow and reduce strain on the heart. Additionally, it may also help alleviate symptoms such as swelling and shortness of breath associated with congestive heart failure, providing relief and improving quality of life for those affected.
remedy_constituents: Boswellic Acids: These triterpenoid compounds, particularly acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA), may help alleviate congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, thereby improving cardiovascular function and reducing symptoms.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as α-pinene and β-pinene, present in Boswellia serrata, may contribute to its cardiovascular benefits by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate cardiac injury and dysfunction associated with congestive heart failure.

β-Sitosterol: This phytosterol may help congestive heart failure by exerting a protective effect on the cardiovascular system, improving lipid profiles, and reducing inflammation, all of which can contribute to improved heart function and reduced risk of cardiac complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 10 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground boswellia serrata powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and add 10 grams of shea butter and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a sterile container and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like hibiscus, turmeric, and ginger contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with congestive heart failure.

Vasodilatory properties: Herbal teas like hawthorn, dandelion, and motherwort have been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help reduce blood pressure and improve blood flow to the heart, making them a potential adjunct therapy for congestive heart failure patients.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidant-rich herbal teas like green tea, chamomile, and lemon balm may help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can contribute to the progression of congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The polyphenols present in Camellia sinensis have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which are key factors in congestive heart failure. Additionally, the cream's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce fluid retention can also contribute to its benefits in managing congestive heart failure. This may help to alleviate symptoms and improve overall heart health.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with congestive heart failure.

Theaflavins: These flavonoids have been shown to have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help improve blood flow and reduce cardiac workload in individuals with congestive heart failure.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, potentially alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain catechins, which have antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and improve heart health.
Roots: They have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat cardiovascular issues, potentially due to their content of flavonoids and alkaloids.
Barks: They may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10g of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 100ml of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt.
Mix 20ml of the cooled camellia sinensis infusion with 10ml of distilled water in a small bowl.
Gradually add the mixture from step 4 to the melted beeswax mixture from step 3 slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The creams also help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced pressure on the heart. Additionally, red clover has been found to lower LDL cholesterol levels and increase HDL cholesterol levels, further supporting overall heart health and reducing the risk of congestive heart failure.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and lowering cholesterol levels, all of which are beneficial for patients with congestive heart failure.

Saponins: The saponin content in red clover cream has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and fluid buildup in the body that is characteristic of congestive heart failure.

Phytoestrogens: Red clover cream's phytoestrogen content may also play a role in helping to regulate blood vessel function and improve cardiac output, both of which are essential for managing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in individuals with congestive heart failure.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and isoflavones, these compounds help to improve blood vessel function and lower blood pressure, making them beneficial for heart health.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality red clover flowers of trifolium pratense species dried and sifted to fine powder form.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered red clover flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and stir well.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 hours under room temperature conditions without direct sunlight exposure.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Mix the resulting herbal liquid extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil to create a smooth and creamy consistency cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The creams' active ingredients, including dong quai root and angelica sinensis, help to relax blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the heart, which can alleviate symptoms such as shortness of breath and fatigue. Additionally, dong quai's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce scarring and fibrosis in the heart tissue, promoting a healthier cardiovascular environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Dong quai's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to the development of congestive heart failure.

Cardiovascular protection: Some studies suggest that dong quai may have cardio-protective effects by improving blood flow, reducing blood pressure, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which could be beneficial for individuals with congestive heart failure.

Anti-fibrotic properties: Dong quai has been shown to possess anti-fibrotic activities, which could help reduce the buildup of scar tissue in the heart muscle that can contribute to congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, Dong Quai roots have been shown to improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation, making them an effective component in medicinal creams for congestive heart failure.

Barks: The bark of the Dong Quai plant contains a unique combination of alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic acids that help to relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and improve circulation, making it a valuable addition to medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica sinensis root and leaf, measuring 30 grams of each for the recipe.
Mix the herbs with sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating it at 60°c for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, squeezing out any excess oil.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax to the mixture, stirring until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 40°c.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, letting it cool and solidify at room temperature before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the body, particularly in the cardiovascular system, which is a hallmark of congestive heart failure. Additionally, licorice creams may also help regulate blood pressure and improve cardiac function by promoting healthy blood vessel dilation and relaxation. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice creams may provide relief for individuals suffering from congestive heart failure.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, common underlying causes of congestive heart failure.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice cream have been shown to improve blood flow and cardiac function by relaxing blood vessels and improving vasodilation, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure such as shortness of breath and swelling.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antihypertensive effects, which can help reduce inflammation and blood pressure in the body, both common contributors to congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used in medicinal creams for congestive heart failure due to their rich content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the heart.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are used in medicinal creams to treat congestive heart failure because they contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and reduce cardiac strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20 drops of rose essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 120°f, then whip it with a hand mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and seal them tightly, storing them in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain potent bioactive compounds that help to reduce fluid buildup in the body, a common symptom of this condition. The cream's diuretic properties, derived from dandelion root and leaf extract, stimulate urine production, allowing excess fluids to be eliminated more efficiently. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help to alleviate symptoms such as swelling and fatigue often associated with congestive heart failure. This natural approach can provide relief for those struggling with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion creams can help increase urine production, reducing fluid buildup in the body and alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure by decreasing pressure on the heart.

Cardio-protective flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in dandelion creams have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to improved heart function.

Potassium content: Dandelion greens are a rich source of potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate blood pressure and promotes healthy cardiac function by counteracting the effects of sodium and reducing fluid retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which helps to reduce inflammation and edema associated with congestive heart failure.
Seeds: Contain a combination of fatty acids and vitamins that help to improve cardiovascular health and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to ensure optimal potency and quality.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days or until crispy and brittle.
Grind 20g of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids, cooling completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with congestive heart failure because they are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. These compounds have been shown to improve cardiovascular function by strengthening blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, blueberry extracts have been found to increase nitric oxide production, which helps to dilate blood vessels and improve cardiac output. As a result, herbal blueberry creams may help to alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure, such as shortness of breath and fatigue, by promoting healthier cardiovascular function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Blueberries contain high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are associated with congestive heart failure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anthocyanins present in blueberries have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system and improve symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Cardioprotective compounds: Blueberries contain various cardioprotective compounds, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and proanthocyanidins, which may help protect against cardiovascular disease by improving blood flow, reducing blood pressure, and preventing the formation of blood clots.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50g of vaccinium myrtillus dried fruits into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 200ml of distilled water with the ground vaccinium myrtillus powder in a heat-resistant glass container for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the solution reaches 60°c to 70°c for 20 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature, then filter it using a coffee filter paper for 15 minutes.
Transfer the filtered liquid into sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The antioxidants present in these creams, such as resveratrol, help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate heart failure symptoms. Additionally, certain herbal extracts in grape creams have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve cardiac function, leading to enhanced circulation and reduced congestion in the heart.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape creams rich in antioxidants like resveratrol and quercetin can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the progression of heart failure.

Vasodilatory properties: The bioactive compounds present in herbal grape creams, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing the workload on the heart.

Inflammation-reducing properties: Herbal grape creams containing anti-inflammatory agents like polyphenols and phenolic acids may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often present in congestive heart failure patients and can worsen symptoms and prognosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Grape roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them beneficial for managing symptoms of congestive heart failure.

Barks: The bark of the grapevine contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry vitis vinifera leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Crush dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of crushed vitis vinifera powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler for 45 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 65 degrees celsius under continuous stirring for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool at room temperature before use within 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain beta-glucan, a soluble fiber that has been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The cream also contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in herbs such as hibiscus, chamomile, and lavender, which can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of oats creams can help soothe dry, cracked skin often associated with congestive heart failure. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, oats creams may help alleviate symptoms and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oats creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and saponins can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure such as swelling and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal oats creams, including polyphenols and flavonoids, can help protect the heart and blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of congestive heart failure.

Cardiac glycoside-like effects: Herbal oats creams containing cardiac glycosides like avenacosides may help regulate heart rhythm and improve cardiac output in individuals with congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with congestive heart failure.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiac function, and prevent platelet aggregation.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by autoclaving them for 20 minutes at 121 degrees celsius.
Infuse 500 grams of dried avena sativa aerial parts in 1000 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours at 65 degrees celsius.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a stainless steel container and discard the solids then add 20% by weight of sweet almond oil.
Mix the resulting herbal extract with 30% by weight of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes stirring occasionally until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool to 40 degrees celsius before pouring it into the sterilized glass jars and sealing them tightly.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for congestive heart failure are topical treatments derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms of this condition by improving cardiovascular function, reducing inflammation, and enhancing overall heart health. The best herbal creams for congestive heart failure include Digitalis purpurea, Crataegus monogyna, Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Astragalus membranaceus, Lavandula angustifolia, Ruscus aculeatus, Centella asiatica, and Withania somnifera. If left untreated, congestive heart failure can lead to shortness of breath, fatigue, swelling, and even heart failure, severely impacting daily life and causing anxiety, decreased mobility, and reduced quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to congestive heart failure that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's phenomenon, and poor circulation. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, hawthorn, and horse chestnut may help improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as cold hands and feet, numbness, and pain. These natural remedies can provide an alternative or complementary approach to traditional treatments for these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: I can't answer that. If you are experiencing congestive heart failure, please seek advice from a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Digitalis purpurea creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain cardiotonic glycosides, specifically digoxin, which increases the strength of cardiac contractions and reduces the heart's workload. This leads to improved circulation, allowing the heart to pump more efficiently. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Digitalis purpurea may help reduce edema, a common complication of congestive heart failure. By alleviating these symptoms, Digitalis purpurea creams can provide relief for individuals with congestive heart failure, improving their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: These compounds increase the contractility of the heart, allowing it to pump blood more efficiently, which is beneficial in congestive heart failure.
Cardiac glycosides: Similar to glycosides, cardiac glycosides also increase the contractility of the heart and help reduce the heart's workload, making it easier to manage congestive heart failure.

Bufadienolides: These compounds have a positive inotropic effect, meaning they increase the heart's contractility and help regulate heart rhythm, which is essential in managing congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they contain digoxin, a cardiac glycoside that helps regulate heart rhythm and improve heart function.
Roots: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they also contain digoxin and other cardiac glycosides that have similar effects.
Stems: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they may contain some cardiac glycosides, although the amounts are generally lower than in leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest digitalis purpurea flowers at peak potency in mid to late summer when the buds are just beginning to open.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area at a temperature not exceeding 30 degrees celsius for at least two weeks.
Combine dried flowers with 20 grams of base cream and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a glass jar for storage and later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna creams helps with congestive heart failure because they are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to improve cardiovascular function by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which dilates blood vessels and improves blood flow. Additionally, Crataegus monogyna creams have been shown to enhance cardiac contractility and reduce inflammation in the heart, leading to improved symptoms and quality of life for individuals with congestive heart failure. Regular use may also help to reduce the risk of heart failure exacerbation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with congestive heart failure by improving cardiac function, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the heart's ability to pump blood efficiently.
Phenolic acids: They contribute to congestive heart failure management by reducing oxidative stress, lowering blood pressure, and improving blood vessel function.
Triterpenes: They aid in congestive heart failure treatment by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thereby reducing cardiac damage and improving overall heart health.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve cardiovascular function.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation.
Barks: They are used to make creams for congestive heart failure because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Steep the dried leaves and flowers in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 10% of the strained oil with beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Pour the cooled mixture into a jar and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with congestive heart failure because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The extracts from Ginkgo biloba leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to dilate blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the heart. This improved circulation helps to reduce symptoms of heart failure, such as fatigue and shortness of breath, by allowing the heart to function more efficiently. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that help to protect the heart from damage and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These constituents help alleviate congestive heart failure by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that protect the heart from oxidative damage.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba may help with congestive heart failure by improving cardiac function, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting anti-apoptotic properties that protect the heart from cell death.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, may help with congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate heart failure symptoms.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects that can help improve blood flow and reduce heart failure symptoms.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the heart and blood vessels from damage.
remedy_recipe: Extract 40 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture to remove the solids and strain it through a coffee filter paper.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil with the filtered liquid in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix well until it reaches a uniform consistency.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and set for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its adaptogenic properties that promote cardiovascular health. It increases nitric oxide production, which relaxes and dilates blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, ginseng's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, protecting against cellular damage and promoting overall heart health. This results in improved cardiac function and reduced symptoms of congestive heart failure. Regular use may also enhance endurance and reduce fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: They help with congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heart failure.
Terpenoids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the heart from damage and reduce the risk of heart failure.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help improve blood vessel function and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are a rich source of ginsenosides, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health and reduce symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Stems: The stems of Panax ginseng contain compounds that may help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, contributing to the management of congestive heart failure symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Panax ginseng have been traditionally used to enhance cardiovascular health and may be used in creams to help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Steam 200g of panax ginseng roots in 1 liter of water for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Grind the steamed roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 50g of ginseng powder with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 20ml of distilled water and 10ml of glycerin to the mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extract in the cream has been shown to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart failure. Additionally, the cream's ability to regulate cortisol levels can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for heart failure episodes. By reducing inflammation and promoting overall cardiovascular health, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure and improve patient outcomes.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with congestive heart failure by improving heart function, reducing inflammation, and preventing oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart failure.
Flavonoids: They help with congestive heart failure by exerting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce the workload on the heart.

Licoricidin (Glycyrrhizin): It helps with congestive heart failure by reducing inflammation, improving heart function, and preventing oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart failure, in addition to its diuretic properties that help reduce fluid buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in traditional medicine, possibly due to their ability to provide antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in herbal medicine, possibly due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and clean them thoroughly under running water for 2 minutes.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the powder with 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Add 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of coconut oil to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams help with congestive heart failure because they contain compounds that may improve cardiovascular function and reduce inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate fluid buildup and swelling in the heart and surrounding tissues. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been traditionally used to enhance the body's ability to adapt to stress, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing the physical strain of congestive heart failure. This natural approach may help alleviate symptoms and improve overall well-being. Regular application may promote a healthier heart and reduce strain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help reduce inflammation and improve heart function by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enhancing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thus alleviating congestive heart failure symptoms.

Polysaccharides: Stimulate the immune system and improve heart function by increasing the production of cytokines and activating immune cells, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with congestive heart failure.

Flavonoids: Exert antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the heart, thereby alleviating congestive heart failure symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus are used for their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Astragalus membranaceus are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus root from a trusted supplier or your own cultivation after 2 years of growth.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes or until the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with congestive heart failure because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress, two key factors contributing to heart failure. The calming effects of the creams can help lower blood pressure and heart rate, alleviating the strain on the heart. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Lavandula angustifolia may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, further supporting heart health and potentially reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to congestive heart failure by lowering blood pressure and reducing the heart rate.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to congestive heart failure.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are commonly used to make creams due to their calming and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used in creams for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are sometimes used in creams for their calming and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Infuse the dried flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the solids from the oil.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of a moisturizing base such as coconut oil and shea butter.
Whip the mixture until it becomes a smooth and consistent cream using a hand mixer for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain bioactive compounds that can help to alleviate edema and swelling associated with the condition. These compounds, such as ruscogenin and alstoscholarin, have been shown to have a diuretic effect, promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body and reducing pressure on the heart. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus creams may help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can further support heart health and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_constituents: Furostanol Saponins: They help alleviate congestive heart failure by reducing peripheral vascular resistance, which in turn decreases blood pressure and improves cardiac output.

Aculeatin: It helps with congestive heart failure by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system.

Saponins: They help alleviate congestive heart failure by exhibiting a positive inotropic effect (increasing the contractility of the heart) and improving microcirculation, thus enhancing the overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ruscus aculeatus leaves are used in herbal medicine as they contain saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Ruscus aculeatus roots are used in herbal medicine due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.

(No third part could be verified to be used for congestive heart failure, but) Stems: Ruscus aculeatus stems are also used in herbal medicine as they are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and 50g of sweet almond oil in a clean room.
Infuse 100g of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves in 500ml of sweet almond oil for 2 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil to the infused mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with congestive heart failure because they are rich in antioxidants and triterpenoid compounds that improve cardiovascular function. These compounds enhance blood flow and reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate congestion and edema. Additionally, Centella asiatica has been shown to improve endothelial function, reducing vascular resistance and improving cardiac output. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce oxidative stress, making it a potential adjunctive therapy for managing congestive heart failure symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help improve heart function by enhancing the expression of genes involved in cardiac function and reducing inflammation in the heart.
Asiatic acid: Exert a protective effect on the heart by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which contribute to heart failure.
Madecassoside: Enhance angiogenesis (formation of new blood vessels) and reduce fibrosis (scarring) in the heart, improving its pumping efficiency and reducing symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties, which help alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
Stems: Employed for their ability to enhance cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation, contributing to the management of congestive heart failure.
Roots: Utilized due to their potential to improve heart function, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of congestive heart failure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried centella asiatica leaves and soak them in 200ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Steep the soaked centella asiatica mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to release its active ingredients.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the extract from the solids.
Mix the strained extract with 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Stir the mixture over low heat until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams helps with congestive heart failure because they contain compounds that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, two key factors contributing to the condition. The antioxidant properties in Withania somnifera help protect the heart cells from damage, promoting overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, the adaptogenic properties of Withania somnifera help the body adapt to physical and emotional stress, improving cardiovascular function and reducing symptoms associated with congestive heart failure. This natural approach may also enhance heart function and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Withanone: Withanone helps with congestive heart failure by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to heart damage.
Withaferin A: Withaferin A may help with congestive heart failure by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Alkaloids (Withanoside and Withanolid):  Alkaloids in Withania somnifera may help with congestive heart failure by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which can help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are commonly used due to their high concentration of withanolides, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for heart health.
Leaves: Withania somnifera leaves are used to create creams for congestive heart failure due to their adaptogenic properties, which help reduce stress and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.

Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are used in creams for congestive heart failure as they contain steroidal saponins, which have been shown to have potential cardiovascular benefits, including lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried withania somnifera root and 20g of coconut oil in the early morning hours.
Combine the dried root in 1 cup of distilled water and bring to a boil then simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture and discard the solids then add in 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler then slowly add in 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of distilled water.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a smooth consistency then transfer to an airtight container.
